items.title: AI Chip Rally Lifts Wall Street Despite Rising Oil Prices
items.desc: The benchmark S&P 500 rose 0.9%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 372 points, or 0.7%, with about an hour left in trading. The Nasdaq Composite outperformed, climbing 1.3%, led by technology shares.

items.title: Syria Begins Steps to Establish Consular Presence in Erbil
items.desc: An official Syrian delegation has traveled to the Kurdistan Region to prepare for the opening of Syria's consulate in Erbil, aiming to expand consular services for Syrian citizens living in the Region.

items.title: Erbil International Airport Pauses Operations Following Drone Attack
items.desc: Erbil International Airport has resumed flight operations after a precautionary suspension of nearly one hour prompted by the security situation.

items.title: Coalition Forces Intercept Six Explosive Drones Over Erbil
items.desc: The drones destroyed over Erbil caused no immediately reported casualties or damage.

items.title: Tourism Received $7.68 Billion of Kurdistan Region's $22 Billion Investment Portfolio
items.desc: Official figures show tourism accounted for $7.68 billion of more than $22 billion invested across the Kurdistan Region over the past seven years, underscoring the sector's central role in economic development.

items.title: Displaced Ras al-Ain families prepare to return home
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Hasakah Preparations are underway to organize the first group of displaced residents from the Syrian city of Ras al-Ain (Serekaniye) and its surrounding countryside to return to their homes in southeastern rural areas of the city, the UK-based Syrian Observatory for Human Rights (SOHR) reported on Tuesday. items.title: Houthi Saudi-port blockade diverts six ships
items.desc: Shafaq News- Sanaa Six commercial vessels turned back in the Red Sea over the past 24 hours after Yemen’s Houthis (Ansarallah) threatened to target ships violating their newly declared maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia, Houthi-affiliated media, citing a military source, reported on Tuesday. The blockade, announced on Monday, bars international shipping companies from loading or unloading car

items.title: Missiles target Iranian Kurdish opposition base in Erbil
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil (Updated at 21:55) Air defense systems intercepted two ballistic missiles fired at a base belonging to the Iranian Kurdish opposition group Kurdistan Freedom Party (PAK) on the outskirts of Erbil, the capital of Iraqi Kurdistan, a security source told Shafaq News on Tuesday. items.title: Four drones destroyed near US Consulate in Erbil
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil (Updated at 21:58) Air defenses protecting the US Consulate in Erbil, the capital of Iraqi Kurdistan, intercepted four drones heading toward the compound on Tuesday, a security source told Shafaq News. All four were destroyed before reaching their destination, preventing casualties and damage. Authorities are investigating the incident. No further details were immediately availa

items.title: US threatens Iran with “ten times harder” strikes
items.desc: Shafaq News- Washington US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth on Tuesday warned Iran it would face a military response “ten times harder” if it continued attacking commercial shipping and countries across the region. items.title: Carney looking at 'all options' as Trump announces 50% tariffs on Canada
items.desc: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

items.title: 'Software error' led to almost 400 non-citizens voting in New Jersey, governor says
items.desc: The department of motor vehicles wrongly registered 6,600 non-Americans to vote, despite their indicating they were not US citizens.

items.title: Trial opens for US mum who says postpartum issues led her to killing own children
items.desc: Lindsay Clancy's lawyer and her husband say she suffered hallucinations and delusions after giving birth.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.desc: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

items.title: Watch rescuers save missing dog from San Francisco Bay
items.desc: Rescuers in boats race toward the dog before making a one-handed grab to pluck the pup out of the water.

items.title: Nicaragua's President Ortega says no more elections, extending his 20-year rule
items.desc: The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.desc: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
items.desc: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

items.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthi rebels threaten to blockade another key strait
items.desc: The Houthis, a Shia militia in Yemen that's aligned with Iran, have announced a naval blockade of a key waterway, the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

items.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
items.desc: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.

items.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 12, including family of six, medics say
items.desc: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in one attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.desc: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.title: Why Iran is risking so much over the Strait of Hormuz
items.desc: None of the choices facing Iran’s leaders offer a clear path to victory, as the conflict with the US continues.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.desc: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.
